title: Inspect /Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public for how Kernel and /vote are currently positioned. Focus on CODEX.md, README.md, .codex/prompts/kernel.md, .codex/prompts/vote.md, scripts/check-codex-kernel-prompt.sh, scripts/check-codex-vote-prompt.sh, scripts/local/launchers/*. Return the min...
summary_text: 1. **High – Missing dedupe for non-terminal sources in fallback backup path** - `scripts/local/run-kernel-task-completion-backup.sh:68-77` only recognizes terminal sources `kernel-progress-save|kernel-phase-complete|kernel-run-complete` for local dedupe, and `recent_terminal_completion_exists` is hard-coded to only accept `kernel-run-complete|fugue-run-complete` (line `83-91`). - In practice, this means `kernel-phase-complete` and `kernel-progress-save` records can bypass dedupe when the external backup tool is unavailable (and fallback branch is used), because `recent_terminal_completion_exists` is only invoked for terminal dedupe and does not apply to those sources. - Impact: repeated phase/checkpoint invocations can emit duplicate `kernel-task-completion-backup` dispatches to GitHub Actions on retries/replays. 2. **High – Duplicate dispatch risk when external tool is unavailable (or not installed)** - The main dedupe path in `run-kernel-task-completion-backup.sh` is delegated to `codex_kernel_guard` when `EXTERNAL_BACKUP_TOOL_AVAILABLE=true` (lines `24-26`, `321-381`). - If that tool is absent, fallback writes a payload, appends journal immediately, then dispatches via raw `gh api` without any record-level idempotency key check in addition to journal entry (lines `383-392`). - Impact: each script execution becomes an independent dispatch event unless it happens to fall under the narrow terminal cooldown branch, so fallback mode is prone to duplicate GHA dispatches under retries. 3. **Medium – “retry noise” can still duplicate terminal dispatches** - Fallback dedupe for terminal sources (`kernel-run-complete`) is only time-window based (`TERMINAL_COMPLETION_DEDUPE_SEC=300`, lines `16`, `94-143`) and does not anchor on a persistent event ID. - Re-running the same completion after >300s can generate a second dispatch even if semantically same completion event. - Impact: bounded suppression exists, but it is only a cooldown window, not strict event-level idempotency.
